title: Enhanced Sign-in Security in Windows
license_note: Screenshots from support.microsoft.com. Confirm redistribution with your legal team before use in a shipped product.

steps.text: In the Settings app on your Windows device, select Accounts > Sign-in options or use the following shortcut: ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Sign-in options ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

On devices running Windows 11, version 24H2 or newer : Under Additional settings > Enhanced sign-in security , there‚Äôs a toggle that allows you to enable or disable ESS if you have relevant sensors available. When the toggle is Off , ESS is disabled and you can use non-ESS Windows Hello compatible peripherals to sign in.

When the toggle is On , ESS is enabled and you can‚Äôt use non-ESS Windows Hello compatible peripherals to sign in. Remember, you can still use external peripherals within apps like Teams when ESS is On.

If you have no toggle but see Enhanced sign-in security available as set up , the first sensor you enroll will determine your ESS state. For example, if you first enroll an ESS sensor, you will have ESS On. If you enroll a non-ESS sensor first, you will have ESS Off.

On devices running Windows 11, version 23H2 : Under Additional settings > Sign in with an external camera or fingerprint reader , there's a toggle that allows you to enable or disable ESS: When the toggle is Off , ESS is enabled and you may not be able to use external peripherals to sign in. Remember, you can still use external peripherals within apps like Teams
When the toggle is On , ESS is disabled and you can use Windows Hello compatible peripherals to sign in
